articles.title: Stocks Drop as Oil Tops $100 and Goldman Slides: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Oil jumped above $100 a barrel while stocks and bonds fell after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following the weekend’s deadlock in US-Iran peace talks. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares dropped in an underwhelming start to the earnings season.
articles.author: Andre Janse Van Vuuren, Julien Ponthus
articles.content_text: Stocks Drop as Oil Tops $100 and Goldman Slides: Markets Wrap Oil jumped above $100 a barrel while stocks and bonds fell after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following the weekend’s deadlock in US-Iran peace talks. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares dropped in an underwhelming start to the earnings season.

articles.title: Hungary’s Magyar Calls for Swift Transition of Power After Vote
articles.summary: Peter Magyar called on Hungary’s president to ensure a swift transition of power after Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party suffered a landslide election loss on Sunday.
articles.author: Thomas Escritt, Marton Kasnyik
articles.content_text: Hungary’s Magyar Calls for Swift Transition of Power After Vote Peter Magyar called on Hungary’s president to ensure a swift transition of power after Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s party suffered a landslide election loss on Sunday.

articles.title: Lafarge Fined €5.7 Million, Ex-CEO Jailed for Terrorist Payments
articles.summary: Holcim Ltd.’s Lafarge was fined nearly €5.7 million ($6.7 million) and its former chief executive was jailed for 6 years after being found guilty of making payments to terrorist organizations to keep open a cement plant during the civil war in Syria.
articles.author: Gaspard Sebag
articles.content_text: Lafarge Fined €5.7 Million, Ex-CEO Jailed for Terrorist Payments Holcim Ltd.’s Lafarge was fined nearly €5.7 million ($6.7 million) and its former chief executive was jailed for 6 years after being found guilty of making payments to terrorist organizations to keep open a cement plant during the civil war in Syria.

articles.title: Goldman Bond Traders Miss Even as Stocks Team Beats Record
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time quarterly high by more than $1 billion, as the war in Iran fueled market volatility, offsetting a disappointing haul by their fixed-income colleagues.
articles.author: Todd Gillespie
articles.content_text: Goldman Bond Traders Miss Even as Stocks Team Beats Record Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time quarterly high by more than $1 billion, as the war in Iran fueled market volatility, offsetting a disappointing haul by their fixed-income colleagues.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley Says Earnings Shield S&P 500 From Iran War
articles.summary: Accelerating earnings are protecting the S&P 500 from deeper losses and masking a broader pullback in US equities, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley.
articles.author: Levin Stamm
articles.content_text: Morgan Stanley Says Earnings Shield S&P 500 From Iran War Accelerating earnings are protecting the S&P 500 from deeper losses and masking a broader pullback in US equities, according to strategists at Morgan Stanley.

articles.title: How Three Key Ships Moved Through Hormuz Hours Ahead of US Blockade
articles.summary: Here's how news of a US Navy blockade affected the movement of three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's David Ingles reports. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: How Three Key Ships Moved Through Hormuz Hours Ahead of US Blockade Here's how news of a US Navy blockade affected the movement of three ships in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's David Ingles reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: HSBC Said to Lose Second Dubai Bond Banker to Wall Street Rival
articles.summary: A senior Dubai-based bond banker at HSBC Holdings Plc is moving to Bank of America Corp., marking the second departure from the British lender in recent days as rivals continue to bolster staffing despite uncertainty stemming from the Iran war.
articles.author: Laura Gardner Cuesta
articles.content_text: HSBC Said to Lose Second Dubai Bond Banker to Wall Street Rival A senior Dubai-based bond banker at HSBC Holdings Plc is moving to Bank of America Corp., marking the second departure from the British lender in recent days as rivals continue to bolster staffing despite uncertainty stemming from the Iran war.

articles.title: OPEC Output Suffers Record Plunge as Iran War Throttles Exports
articles.summary: OPEC crude production registered a record plunge last month as conflict in the Middle East throttled exports from key members, the group’s data showed.
articles.author: Grant Smith
articles.content_text: OPEC Output Suffers Record Plunge as Iran War Throttles Exports OPEC crude production registered a record plunge last month as conflict in the Middle East throttled exports from key members, the group’s data showed.

articles.title: Stock Market Trading Shows Fear of Risk Despite Weeks of Gains
articles.summary: On the surface, it appears that the stock market has been on a tear for the past couple of weeks. Look under the hood, however, and you’ll see that investors are buying but without the enthusiasm that usually marks the start of a major rally.
articles.author: Alexandra Semenova, Youkyung Lee
articles.content_text: Stock Market Trading Shows Fear of Risk Despite Weeks of Gains On the surface, it appears that the stock market has been on a tear for the past couple of weeks. Look under the hood, however, and you’ll see that investors are buying but without the enthusiasm that usually marks the start of a major rally.

articles.title: US Threat to Block Iranian Oil Faces Practical Obstacles
articles.summary: Plans to obstruct the waterway threaten to deepen the energy supply shock.
articles.author: Grant Smith
articles.content_text: US Threat to Block Iranian Oil Faces Practical Obstacles Plans to obstruct the waterway threaten to deepen the energy supply shock.

articles.title: Iran Threatens Ports as Trump Vows Hormuz Blockade
articles.summary: Iran warned it would target all ports in and close to the Persian Gulf if its own shipping hubs are threatened, after US President Donald Trump announced plans for a blockade of Tehran-linked vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: Iran Threatens Ports as Trump Vows Hormuz Blockade Iran warned it would target all ports in and close to the Persian Gulf if its own shipping hubs are threatened, after US President Donald Trump announced plans for a blockade of Tehran-linked vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Premarket Movers: Baker Hughes, Goldman, Leggett, Replimune
articles.summary: S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.7% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following a deadlock in peace talks with Iran.
articles.author: Will Daley
articles.content_text: US Premarket Movers: Baker Hughes, Goldman, Leggett, Replimune S&P 500 Index futures are down 0.7% as of 7:50 a.m. in New York after President Donald Trump ordered a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz following a deadlock in peace talks with Iran.

articles.title: Orban and Aides Vanish From Sight After Hungarian Election Loss
articles.summary: Viktor Orban and his top lieutenants dominated Hungary’s airwaves over 16 years of his rule. But since last night’s landslide election, they are nowhere to be found.
articles.author: Thomas Escritt
articles.content_text: Orban and Aides Vanish From Sight After Hungarian Election Loss Viktor Orban and his top lieutenants dominated Hungary’s airwaves over 16 years of his rule. But since last night’s landslide election, they are nowhere to be found.

articles.title: Big US Banks Set to Temper Bond Sales After Stout 2026 Debut
articles.summary: The six largest US banks are expected to issue fewer bonds this quarter after the busiest start of a year on record.
articles.author: Yash Roy
articles.content_text: Big US Banks Set to Temper Bond Sales After Stout 2026 Debut The six largest US banks are expected to issue fewer bonds this quarter after the busiest start of a year on record.

articles.title: Ackman’s Joint Pershing Square, Fund IPO Begins Marketing
articles.summary: Bill Ackman has kicked off formal marketing for the US initial public offering of his closed-end fund and his hedge fund, even as the Iran war hits dealmaking optimism.
articles.author: Bailey Lipschultz, Georgie McKay
articles.content_text: Ackman’s Joint Pershing Square, Fund IPO Begins Marketing Bill Ackman has kicked off formal marketing for the US initial public offering of his closed-end fund and his hedge fund, even as the Iran war hits dealmaking optimism.

articles.title: Hungarian Stocks and Forint Jump as Orban Loss Starts New Era
articles.summary: Hungary’s forint surged to a four-year high and local stocks jumped to a record after Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s election defeat. The opposition’s landslide win is buoying markets on the expectation that the result will help unlock European Union funding and revive the economy.
articles.author: Andras Gergely, Marton Kasnyik, Selcuk Gokoluk
articles.content_text: Hungarian Stocks and Forint Jump as Orban Loss Starts New Era Hungary’s forint surged to a four-year high and local stocks jumped to a record after Prime Minister Viktor Orban’s election defeat. The opposition’s landslide win is buoying markets on the expectation that the result will help unlock European Union funding and revive the economy.

articles.title: Goldman Stock Traders Post Second Consecutive Quarterly Record
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Group’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time high by more than $1 billion, reporting revenue of $5.33 billion for the first three months of the year. Dani Burger reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: Goldman Stock Traders Post Second Consecutive Quarterly Record Goldman Sachs Group’s equity traders beat their own previous all-time high by more than $1 billion, reporting revenue of $5.33 billion for the first three months of the year. Dani Burger reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: India’s Inflation Picks Up as Iran War Lifts Energy Costs
articles.summary: India’s inflation edged up in March as the war in the Middle East lifted crude prices and squeezed gas supplies for key industries.
articles.author: Anup Roy
articles.content_text: India’s Inflation Picks Up as Iran War Lifts Energy Costs India’s inflation edged up in March as the war in the Middle East lifted crude prices and squeezed gas supplies for key industries.

articles.title: India’s Shapoorji Seeks to Delay Paying $1.5 Billion of Debt
articles.summary: Shapoorji Pallonji Group, the Indian conglomerate behind the nation’s biggest-ever private credit deal, is trying to delay paying a separate debt, people familiar with the matter said, underscoring mounting strains amid volatile markets.
articles.author: Dipika Lalwani, Saikat Das
articles.content_text: India’s Shapoorji Seeks to Delay Paying $1.5 Billion of Debt Shapoorji Pallonji Group, the Indian conglomerate behind the nation’s biggest-ever private credit deal, is trying to delay paying a separate debt, people familiar with the matter said, underscoring mounting strains amid volatile markets.

articles.title: Harris’ 26North Raises $6 Billion for Debut Private Equity Fund
articles.summary: Josh Harris’ 26North Partners collected almost $6 billion for its debut private equity strategy, a US record for a first-time fundraising.
articles.author: Laura Benitez
articles.content_text: Harris’ 26North Raises $6 Billion for Debut Private Equity Fund Josh Harris’ 26North Partners collected almost $6 billion for its debut private equity strategy, a US record for a first-time fundraising.

articles.title: Iran War: Trump Vows to Blockade Hormuz & Hungary's Orban Concedes Defeat | The Pulse 4/13
articles.summary: "The Pulse With Francine Lacqua" is all about conversations with high profile guests in the beating heart of global business, economics, finance and politics. Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops. Today's guests: Dan Scott, Chief Investment Officer and Head of Multi Asset at Vontobel Asset Management, Sherrill Stroschein, Politics Professor at University College London & Elisabeth Braw, Senior Fellow at the Atlantic Council. (Source: Bloomberg)

Based in London, we go wherever the story is, bringing you exclusive interviews and market-moving scoops. articles.title: China Credit Slowdown Worse Than Forecast as Loan Demand Weakens
articles.summary: China’s credit expansion slowed more than expected from a year earlier, as continued weakness in household and business demand holds back borrowing.
articles.author: Charlie Zhu, Yujing Liu
articles.content_text: China Credit Slowdown Worse Than Forecast as Loan Demand Weakens China’s credit expansion slowed more than expected from a year earlier, as continued weakness in household and business demand holds back borrowing.

articles.title: Oil Rallies as US Vow to Blockade Iran Heightens Supply Risks
articles.summary: Oil jumped as the US vowed to blockade all vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz that called at Iran ports or were headed there, reigniting risks to energy supplies in the region.
articles.author: Alex Longley, Charlie Zhu, Sarah Chen
articles.content_text: Oil Rallies as US Vow to Blockade Iran Heightens Supply Risks Oil jumped as the US vowed to blockade all vessels passing through the Strait of Hormuz that called at Iran ports or were headed there, reigniting risks to energy supplies in the region.

articles.title: Oil Rises as Trump Threatens to Blockade Hormuz; Bank Earnings Kick Off | Bloomberg Brief 4/13/2026
articles.summary: Global stocks and bonds slide as oil rallies back above $100 a barrel as President Trump vows to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and Iran warns that ports in and around the Persian Gulf will not be safe. Goldman Sachs set to kick off bank earnings. Ella Gude of BNY Investments Newton sees pressure for yields to persist. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: Oil Rises as Trump Threatens to Blockade Hormuz; Bank Earnings Kick Off | Bloomberg Brief 4/13/2026 Global stocks and bonds slide as oil rallies back above $100 a barrel as President Trump vows to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and Iran warns that ports in and around the Persian Gulf will not be safe. Goldman Sachs set to kick off bank earnings. Ella Gude of BNY Investments Newton sees pressure for yields to persist. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Indonesia Hikes Nickel Ore Benchmark Price in Hit to Processors
articles.summary: Indonesia will hike benchmark prices for nickel ore, further weighing on local processors facing cost pressures from the Middle East war.
articles.author: Eddie Spence
articles.content_text: Indonesia Hikes Nickel Ore Benchmark Price in Hit to Processors Indonesia will hike benchmark prices for nickel ore, further weighing on local processors facing cost pressures from the Middle East war.

articles.title: Mike Pyle on Building a Portfolio
articles.summary: Barry speaks with Mike Pyle, Senior Managing Director, is the Deputy Head of BlackRock’s Portfolio Management Group (PMG). They discuss the durable economic shocks that could result from the war with Iran including energy security. They also discuss his time in the Biden administration as Deputy National Security Advisor for International Economics. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: Mike Pyle on Building a Portfolio Barry speaks with Mike Pyle, Senior Managing Director, is the Deputy Head of BlackRock’s Portfolio Management Group (PMG). They discuss the durable economic shocks that could result from the war with Iran including energy security. They also discuss his time in the Biden administration as Deputy National Security Advisor for International Economics. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Where Did All the Affordable Homes Go?
articles.summary: Smaller, more affordable homes for first-time buyers have all but disappeared. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Scott Lincicome explains who’s to blame. (Source: Bloomberg)
articles.content_text: Where Did All the Affordable Homes Go? Smaller, more affordable homes for first-time buyers have all but disappeared. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Scott Lincicome explains who’s to blame. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Adams Street Raises $7.5 Billion for Third Private Credit Fund
articles.summary: Adams Street Partners LLC has raised $7.5 billion for its third private credit vehicle focused on institutional investors, more than double the size of its previous fund, even as the market grapples with a retail exodus.
articles.author: Paula Seligson
articles.content_text: Adams Street Raises $7.5 Billion for Third Private Credit Fund Adams Street Partners LLC has raised $7.5 billion for its third private credit vehicle focused on institutional investors, more than double the size of its previous fund, even as the market grapples with a retail exodus.

articles.title: Aluminum Jumps to Four-Year High on Trump’s Blockade of Hormuz
articles.summary: Aluminum jumped to a four-year high in London, as US President Donald Trump’s blockade of Iranian ports threatened more disruptions to shipments from the Persian Gulf.
articles.author: Martin Ritchie
articles.content_text: Aluminum Jumps to Four-Year High on Trump’s Blockade of Hormuz Aluminum jumped to a four-year high in London, as US President Donald Trump’s blockade of Iranian ports threatened more disruptions to shipments from the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: ECB’s Vujcic Says Energy Prices Are Still Very Close to Baseline
articles.summary: European Central Bank Governing Council member Boris Vujcic said energy costs remain near the base-case scenario, despite rising since the recent cease-fire.
articles.author: Jasmina Kuzmanovic
articles.content_text: ECB’s Vujcic Says Energy Prices Are Still Very Close to Baseline European Central Bank Governing Council member Boris Vujcic said energy costs remain near the base-case scenario, despite rising since the recent cease-fire.
